title: Network Methods for Diagonal Integration of Unpaired Single-Cell Multi-Omics Data: A Review.
snippet: Advances in single-cell sequencing have enabled multi-omics profiling at unprecedented resolution; however, mass spectrometry-based single-cell proteomics (scMS) remains inherently destructive, precluding simultaneous transcriptomic capture. Unlike antibody-based methods such as CITE-seq, which permit paired profiling but are restricted to targeted protein....

title: Author Correction: Community benchmarking and evaluation of human unannotated microprotein detection by mass spectrometry based proteomics.
Structural basis for uracil removal from DNA by human SMUG1.
snippet: Human single-strand-selective monofunctional uracil DNA glycosylase 1 (hSMUG1) removes uracil, 5-hydroxymethyluracil (5hmU) and 5-fluorouracil (5FU) from DNA, thereby initiating the base excision repair (BER) process. hSMUG1 is important for maintaining genomic integrity and plays a significant role in cancer biology. Here, we present the structures of hSMU...

title: Global burden of enteric infectious diseases, diarrhoeal diseases, and corresponding aetiologies, 1990-2023: a systematic analysis for the Global Burden of Disease Study 2023.
snippet: Enteric infectious diseases claim more than 1 million lives annually and are among the top ten causes of death in children younger than 5 years. Remarkable global investment has been dedicated to enteric infectious disease prevention and control; however, the shifting global health landscape is testing the continuance of progress. title: ALA-PDT Induces Apoptosis in HPV-transformed Cells through mtDNA Release-Mediated Activation of the cGAS-STING Pathway.
snippet: Aminolevulinic acid photodynamic therapy (ALA-PDT) is an effective and non-invasive treatment for HPV-related diseases, but its underlying mechanisms still need further investigation. cGAS-STING signaling pathway takes part in antiviral innate immune defense, however its changes and regulatory mechanisms in ALA-PDT treatment remain unknown.
